title: trifolium × bertrandii
intro: Trifolium × bertrandii, also known as Bertram's clover, is a rare and hybrid legume species native to the Mediterranean region, resulting from the cross-breeding of Trifolium striatum and Trifolium tomentosum. Bertram's clover has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its high content of saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Trifolium × bertrandii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Trifolium × bertrandii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
